title: scrophularia pyrenaica
intro: Scrophularia pyrenaica, also known as Pyrenean figwort, is a perennial plant species in the figwort family Scrophulariaceae, native to the Pyrenees mountains and parts of southern France and northern Spain. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects, which are attributed to its high concentration of iridoid glycosides and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and skin problems, and has also been used as a natural remedy for digestive issues and to boost the immune system. Scrophularia pyrenaica can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusement, and is also used in some traditional herbal remedies, such as salves and ointments, to treat skin conditions and wounds.
